overview: Golgari Midrange seeks to control the early game with efficient disruption and removal while out-grinding opponents through resilient threats and card advantage. It wins by dictating the pace of the match, leveraging graveyard synergy and targeted removal to outvalue slower, combo, or aggressive strategies.
win_conditions: Sentinel of the Nameless City clearing the board, resilient mid-range threats, and graveyard-fueled card advantage.
Win by out-resourcing through removal and threat cycles.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritizing duals/shocks for fixing) and at least one early interaction (Thoughtseize, removal, or Badgermole Cub).
Keep any hand containing Sentinel of the Nameless City if you have 3+ mana sources.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan flood, screw, or hands with only high-cost threats and no early game plan or disruption.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize on a key combo piece, early threat, or removal spell.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast Badgermole Cub or a 2-drop removal spell.
If opening with Sentinel, ensure you have 3 lands and a removal spell or another threat to support it.
Sequence lands to ensure color fixing, prioritizing duals/shocks if available.
mid_game: Preserve Thoughtseize for high-impact targets (combo pieces, key removal, or win conditions).
Use Badgermole Cub's card advantage efficiently by letting it trade with removal or block early threats.
avoid overcommitting if facing graveyard hate.
Commit threats when you have removal ready for their response.
Hold back Sentinel if you lack an answer to their removal or if they have sweepers.
Prioritize trading efficiently rather than overextending into sweepers or counters.
late_game: Sentinel of the Nameless City clearing the board, resilient mid-range threats, and graveyard-fueled card advantage.
Win by out-resourcing through removal and threat cycles.
Go all-in when you have card advantage secured, removal for their potential answers, and they are at low life or have no sweepers.
Commit when you can trade multiple threats for their removal.

sequencing_tips: Badgermole Cub's card advantage is conditional
don't cast it into a sweeper.
Sentinel of the Nameless City is a board wipe in a single card slot but requires three colors
Thoughtseize is your most important spell
save it for game-ending threats or combo pieces.
Avoid overcommitting to the graveyard if facing hate.

matchups.advice: Use Thoughtseize to discard counterspells or key pieces. Cast threats in response to their removal or when they have no mana. Utilize flash or instant-speed interaction if available.
Apply early pressure with Thoughtseize and Badgermole Cub. Don't let them stabilize; remove their mana rocks or key ramp spells. Use Sentinel to exile their biggest threats.
Stabilize with Thoughtseize to disrupt early plays, use removal efficiently, and deploy Sentinel to clear the board. Transition to card advantage once stabilized.
Apply consistent pressure with mid-range threats. Use Thoughtseize to disrupt their hand. Out-grind them with card advantage and removal. Force them to use resources on your threats.
Win the resource war. Prioritize Thoughtseize for their hand disruption and removal. Use Sentinel to exile their key threats. Control the board with efficient trades and graveyard advantage.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Wrath of God, Toxic Deluge), graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Leyline), and combo win conditions.
Note when opponents hold up mana for removal vs. counterspells.
Track graveyard content if facing graveyard-dependent decks.
